input.background: How large a sample is needed to test the SIDS data presented earlier in this chapter with 90% power at alpha=0.05 two sided? We want to detect a mean difference in birth weight (Delta) of 300 grams (about 2/3 pound). Let us use sample standard deviation s(720.0) as a reasonable estimate of sigma.
input.question: Calculate the sample size needed to detect a mean difference in birth weight of 300 grams with 90% power at alpha=0.05 two sided.

output.method: 达到指定概率所需的最小样本量
output.answer: n=60.46. Round up to 61 to ensure adequate power. Because the sample size exceeds 30, there is no need to apply adjustment factor f.

Delta.description: The mean difference in birth weight to be detected, measured in grams.

s.description: The sample standard deviation, used as an estimate of the population standard deviation (sigma).

alpha.description: The significance level for the two-sided hypothesis test.

power.description: The desired statistical power of the test.

sample_size.description: The required sample size to detect the specified mean difference with the given power and significance level.

meta_info.caption: Sample size requirement, one-sample t test (SIDS).
